description_long: This is a dark green, twin-boom military airplane with a central fuselage and two engines mounted on the leading edge of each wing. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with yellow striping and U.S. Air Force insignia on both the wings and fuselage. The airplane features a glazed, forward-mounted cockpit and a tricycle landing gear configuration, with the nose wheel visible beneath the cockpit. The tail section consists of a horizontal stabilizer connecting two vertical stabilizers at the end of each boom, forming an H-tail design. The propellers are positioned at the front of each engine nacelle, and the overall design is reminiscent of World War II-era fighter or reconnaissance aircraft.
description: A dark green, twin-boom military airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, dual front propellers, and U.S. Air Force markings. It features a glazed cockpit, tricycle landing gear, and an H-tail configuration.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin-boom structure, mid-mounted wings, dual propellers, and visible cockpit glazing.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the H-tail configuration, twin booms, and the full wingspan with visible insignia.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the propellers, cockpit, and landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the dual propellers, forward cockpit, and the tricycle landing gear beneath the nose.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the leading edge of each wing with front-facing propellers.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units, all fixed and visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a glazed canopy, likely single or dual-seat.
